clinical_indications: Symptomatic uterine fibroids not responding to medical management
Asymptomatic uterine fibroids greater than or equal to 14 weeks uterine size
Heavy menstrual bleeding with documented failure of medical treatment
Endometriosis with documented failure of medical treatment
Recurrent endometriosis after failed medical/conservative surgical management
Endometrial hyperplasia without atypia with documented failure of medical treatment
CIN II with poor compliance or CIN III
Adnexal masses (need for hysterectomy to be individualised and justified)
Recurrent post-menopausal bleeding (even in the absence of malignancy)

clinical_thresholds.note: Pop-up criteria for eligibility, must be symptomatic
Documented failed prior medical management (including 2-3 months hormone therapy) or documented reasons for not being indicated

preauth.label: Clinical notes clearly indicating reason(s) for hysterectomy including medical management tried for at least 4-6 months (2-3 months hormone therapy) which failed, or reasons if not indicated
Lab investigations (Complete Blood count, Blood sugar- fasting and post prandial, Renal function test, liver function test, Urine- routine and microscopy)
Pap smear & Cervical biopsy (Essential only in hysterectomy cases for benign cervical conditions)
Documentary evidence of appropriate counselling and informed consent form signed by the patient (especially when performing removal of ovaries)

claim.label: Discharge summary with follow up advise
Picture(s) of gross specimen of the Uterus removed
Histopathology report of the specimen removed

ppd_preauth.q: Is patient's age > 40 years? (If <40, are reasons for hysterectomy documented?)
Has hysterectomy been done in the past?
Is non-gravid uterus size > 12 weeks and symptomatic?
Is there documentary evidence of medical management tried for 4-6 months (2-3 months hormone therapy) which failed?
If medical management not indicated, are reasons available?

cpd_claim.q: Does the patient have relevant history of either multiple Uterine Fibroids or Endometriosis or Adenomyosis or severe Dysfunctional Uterine Bleeding or Carcinoma in situ or long standing Pelvic inflammatory disease?

Is there a documentary evidence of medical management tried for at least 4-6 months, of which at least 2-3 months (i.e. 2-3 cycles) should be of hormone therapy and it failed, or it was not indicated, with reason available? If failed, are the documents proving duration of treatment and failure available?
Are intra-operative stills available confirming the use of laparoscopy?

common_queries: Clinical notes lack clear reasons for hysterectomy or documentation of failed medical management.
Mandatory lab investigations (CBC, Blood Sugar, RFT, LFT, Urine R/M) missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.
Electrocardiogram or X-ray chest reports missing at pre-authorization.
USG Abdomen + Pelvis report missing at pre-authorization.
Pap smear or cervical biopsy missing for hysterectomy cases due to benign cervical conditions.
Counselling documentation and informed consent form missing.
Patient's age less than 40 years without documented reasons for hysterectomy or a second opinion from a qualified gynaecologist (if applicable).
Medical management duration or type (hormone therapy) not matching guidelines, or reasons for not indicating medical management are missing/insufficient.
Indoor case papers missing at claim submission.
Detailed operative notes missing or incomplete at claim submission.
Discharge summary with follow-up advice missing or incomplete at claim submission.
Picture(s) of specimen removed (Gross) missing or incomplete at claim submission.
Histopathology report of specimen removed missing at claim submission.
Intra-operative stills for laparoscopy missing or not confirming laparoscopic procedure.
Hospital lacks documented laparoscopic facilities or trained specialists for performing laparoscopic surgeries.

additional_information.special_empanelment: #Laproscopic facilities and trained specialists in laproscopic procedures for performing laproscopic surgeries
clinical_key_pointers: The diagnosis made should be backed by clinical or Ultrasonography (USG) findings and all required reports shall be uploaded.

In women aged less than 40 and/or low parity (</ = 2) it is mandatory to have a second opinion from a qualified gynaecologist. (However, in case of a public hospital, if only 1 (one) gynaecologist is posted then this condition may be relaxed for that public hospital only).

Documentary evidence of medical management tried for at least 4-6 months, of which at least 2-3 months (i.e. 2-3 cycles) should be of hormone therapy and it failed, or it was not indicated, with reasons. If failed, documents proving duration of treatment and failure.
additional_information.pre_op_preparation: Counselling and Informed Consent: Need for hysterectomy, alternative treatment options, risks and benefits, potential complications of the procedure, removal/conservation of ovaries & tubes, route of hysterectomy, possible need for post operative Hormone therapy in selected cases.
additional_information.post_op_care: Discharge summary with operative details, review for histopathology report, report if there is fever, bleeding or any other symptoms, avoid lifting heavy weight for 8 weeks, abstinence for eight weeks, adequate iron and calcium & Vitamin D3 supplements, evaluate need for hormones in very selected patients.
contraindications: Non specific abdominal or pelvic pain
Minor degree of utero vaginal prolapse
Fibroids which are small (less than 5 cm) or Asymptomatic (less than 12 weeks size uterus)
Simple ovarian cyst less than or equal to 5 cm
